sourceMovie.title: Havoc
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Havoc for the same reason it broke through in 2025: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Gareth Evans keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Payback
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Havoc clicked for you, Payback (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Havoc?
faq.answer: Try Payback, The Boondock Saints, The Rip—each shares DNA with Havoc while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Havoc?
faq.answer: Havoc (2025) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Havoc worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
